It is native to South America and is commonly found in the wild in countries such as Uruguay and Argentina. Cichorium montevidense, also known as the Argentine chicory or Cieca, is a perennial plant native to South America, belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Cichorium montevidense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
